basics.name: Minh Huynh Nguyen
basics.label: PhD Student in Computer Science
basics.summary: PhD student at National University of Singapore working on Large Language Models, Multimodality, Natural Language Processing, and AI4Code

work.name: National University of Singapore
work.summary: Working on Large Language Models, Multimodality, Natural Language Processing, and AI4Code under the supervision of Prof. Bryan Kian Hsiang Low
highlights: Published 4+ papers at top venues including ICML and ICSE workshops
Research on Large Language Models and AI4Code

publications.name: TIGER: Bridging the Multimodal Reasoning-Access Gap via Modality Counterfactuals
publications.summary: A novel approach to address the gap between multimodal reasoning capabilities and access to multimodal information through modality counterfactuals.

publications.name: AgileCoder: Dynamic Collaborative Agents for Software Development based on Agile Methodology
publications.summary: A framework for dynamic collaborative agents that follow agile methodology to enhance software development processes.

publications.name: Neural Rankers for Code Generation via Inter-Cluster Modeling
publications.summary: A novel approach to code generation using inter-cluster modeling for improved ranking of generated code.

projects.name: AgileCoder
projects.summary: Dynamic Collaborative Agents for Software Development based on Agile Methodology - A framework enabling multiple AI agents to collaborate effectively following agile software development practices.

projects.name: TIGER
projects.summary: Bridging the Multimodal Reasoning-Access Gap via Modality Counterfactuals - A novel approach to enhance multimodal reasoning capabilities.
